CIPHER.1002.cipherString: We thought we'd avoid the moustache-twisting, two-dimensional antagonist and create one whose motivation is the pain of his past.
CIPHER.1002.question: This quote is from an interview with Bryan Konietzko, one of the creators of Avatar: the Last Airbender. It has been paraphrased and encoded as an Aristocrat. What does it say?

CIPHER.1003.cipherString: Honest Toad was always ready to admit himself in the wrong.
CIPHER.1003.question: This quote is from a very famous children's book series. It has been encoded as a Nihilist cipher with a Polybius key of AMPHIBIOUSNESS and a keyword of GRUMPY. What does it say?
CIPHER.1003.author: The Wind in the Willows

CIPHER.1005.cipherString: But if something funny happens, I can't resist. I have to tell the people.
CIPHER.1005.question: Someone left this encrypted quote scribbled on the chalkboard before class began. It's a quote by Kathy Griffin, written as a Porta using a keyword of COMIC. What does it say?
CIPHER.1005.author: Kathy Griffin

CIPHER.1007.cipherString: But nothing lasts, not even stone, not even despair.
CIPHER.1007.question: You stumble across this encoded text on an old floppy disk labeled “Project Discovery.” It's a quote by Kim Stanley Robinson encoded as a Fractionated Morse. You are told that the quote starts with BUT N. What does it say?
CIPHER.1007.author: Kim Stanley Robinson

CIPHER.1006.cipherString: Hard to find anything lovelier than a tree. They grow at right angles to a tangent of the nominal sphere of the Earth.
CIPHER.1006.question: You found the following carved into the bark of a hollow log. You are told that it's a quote by Bill Nye the Science Guy encoded as an Aristocrat. What does it say?
CIPHER.1006.author: Bill Nye the Science Guy

CIPHER.1004.cipherString: She is a good example where she's like "I gotta be this hero: perfect and amazing at everything" and that’s just not her.
CIPHER.1004.question: This quote is paraphrased from an interview where She-Ra and the Princesses of Power creator ND Stevenson is discussing the main character of the series. What three-word key phrase was used to encode this K3 Aristocrat? It will help to be aware in advance that almost all of the letters of the alphabet are used at least once in this cipher.

CIPHER.1009.cipherString: of all my abilities, the power to make you smile is by far my favorite and i intend on using it recklessly
CIPHER.1009.question: The following poem by JmStorm found on Facebook has been encoded as a Complete Columnar cipher. You are told it contains TEND ON. What does it say?

CIPHER.1008.cipherString: To communicate effectively, avoid overloading your teammates with superfluous information, and ask only for essential details.
CIPHER.1008.question: The following text is a helpful tip from the cooperative game Perspectives. It's been encoded as a K2 Aristocrat. What does it say?

CIPHER.1011.question: A mysterious QR code revealed an Italian proverb encoded as a Hill cipher with a keyword of NERD. What does it say?
CIPHER.1011.author: Italian Proverb

CIPHER.1010.cipherString: A shift is necessary toward lifestyles less geared to environmentally damaging consumption patterns.
CIPHER.1010.question: You intercepted this transmission from a rogue weather balloon. Decode the Aristocrat version of Maurice Strong's famous words using a K2 alphabet. The cipher contains the plaintext letter H occurring only once, in the second word. What was the K2 Keyword used to encode it?
CIPHER.1010.author: Maurice Strong

CIPHER.1012.cipherString: Yes, we'll yell, 'Help, help us, goose girl, and bring the terrifying legion of warrior geese'.
CIPHER.1012.question: Hidden in the source code of a retro video game, this Checkerboard puzzle reveals a quote by a novelist with a polybius key of MIGRATIONS. What does it say?
CIPHER.1012.author: a novelist

CIPHER.1013.cipherString: The nectar’s sweet and the competition is savage.
CIPHER.1013.question: This tag line for a new board game has been encoded as a Fractionated Morse cipher. You are told that it contains COMP, corresponding with the encoded values Z E W M L F T, meaning E = ●–●; F = ––●; L = –×●; M = –×–; T = ×●×; W = ×––; Z = ××–.What does it say?

CIPHER.1014.cipherString: Había gastado todo el dinero en cosas innecesarias y los bancos empezaron a llamarla por las deudas que tenía.
CIPHER.1014.question: The following Spanish-language text about what happened after Rosa won $94 million dollars in the lottery has been encoded as a K2 Xenocrypt with an English keyword. What does it say?

CIPHER.1015.question: A strange code appears in the footer of a document you print from the lab computer. It's encoded as a Cryptarithm. What do the values 305128 9256 decode to?

CIPHER.1016.cipherString: Give me a big enough sandbox to play in, and I'll find the part of it not being used as kitty litter.
CIPHER.1016.question: While repairing an old telescope, you find a note hidden in the tube. The message is a quote by Andy Kessler written in an Aristocrat using a K1 alphabet. What was the K1 Keyword used to encode it?
CIPHER.1016.author: Andy Kessler

CIPHER.1017.cipherString: And the ocean, calling out to us both. A song of freedom and longing.
CIPHER.1017.question: A message in a bottle washed up in the school's decorative fountain—decode this Nihilist Substitution cipher text by Alexandra Christo with a keyword of IMAGERY and polybius key of REJUVENESCENT. What does it say?
CIPHER.1017.author: Alexandra Christo

CIPHER.1018.cipherString: The vaulted ceiling of a bustling medieval courtroom soars above you. The crowd of human-sized playing cards parts.
CIPHER.1018.question: The descriptive quote below is from Puzzling Pursuits' game Down the Rabbit Hole. It's been encoded as an K1 Aristocrat cipher - what does it say?

CIPHER.1019.cipherString: in that creepy spare bedroom down the hall
CIPHER.1019.question: You found these little guys at the end credits of your favorite video game. A closer look reveals them to encode a quote from Level 99's website (it's fun, you should go!). They form a Baconian cipher. What do they say?

CIPHER.1020.question: Etched into the surface of a melted vinyl record, you found this Cryptarithm-encrypted quote. What do the values 1501350 56437 907625393 decode to?

CIPHER.1021.cipherString: This is only a foretaste of what is to come and only the shadow of what is going to be.
CIPHER.1021.question: You receive this cryptic message from the future through an online time capsule. It's a quote by Alan M. Turing, written in a Complete Columnar. The quote has GOING TO somewhere in it. What does it say?
CIPHER.1021.author: Alan M. Turing

CIPHER.1022.cipherString: I wanted one thing - to show the fantastic world created by nature over millions of years. Film offered me that chance.
CIPHER.1022.question: The quote below by Karel Zeman discussing his purpose as a filmmaker has been encoded as a Patristocrat cipher with a two-word K1 key phrase. You are told that it ends with a 4- and a 6-letter word (in that order). There are also four instances of L in the plaintext. What does it say?
CIPHER.1022.author: Karel Zeman

CIPHER.1023.cipherString: My motto when I was writing this was 'replace judgment with curiosity'.
CIPHER.1023.question: Lynn Nottage was heard to say the following, which has been encoded as a Porta using a keyword of FUGUE. What does it say?
CIPHER.1023.author: Lynn Nottage

CIPHER.1028.cipherString: If you make this a timed question the peasants will revolt violently
CIPHER.1028.question: A former Codebusters competitor sent me the following message on Discord. I encoded it as a Checkerboard cipher with a Polybius key of TWELVE THOUSAND - what does it say?

CIPHER.1024.question: Someone left this coded message in the school lab. You are told it's a quote encoded with a Hill with a keyword of LOST. What does it say?

CIPHER.1025.cipherString: optical and microwave sensors for detecting ice
CIPHER.1025.question: This passage, encoded as a Baconian cipher, describes a new invention being developed by the University of Michigan College of Engineering. What does it say?

CIPHER.1026.cipherString: Se presentan los resultados del estudio técnico de treinta obras, entre pinturas y dibujos, que conserva el Museo del Prado.
CIPHER.1026.question: This Spanish-language text comes from the online newsletter of a very famous museum, whose name appears in the passage. It has been encoded as a K1 Xenocrypt for you to solve. What does it say?

CIPHER.1027.cipherString: You've got to think lucky. If you fall into a mudhole, check your back pocket - you might have caught a fish.
CIPHER.1027.question: A coded message is discovered inside a fortune cookie. It turns out to be a quote by Darrell Royal, encoded using a K2 Aristocrat Cipher. What does it say?
CIPHER.1027.author: Darrell Royal

CIPHER.1029.cipherString: five minutes for youtube to chomp on it
CIPHER.1029.question: The strange strings of words below are actually a Baconian cipher, encrypting a bit of text that John Toebes sent me in an email. You are told that the quote ends with ON IT. What does it say?

CIPHER.1030.cipherString: And then music. Wind chimes. And the sound of someone playing a violin. A fiddle. Or maybe just the music of a little dragon's wings.
CIPHER.1030.question: An explorer's “journal of observations" is encoded below. It turns out to be a quote by Christopher Golden, written in a Patristocrat Cipher using a K2 alphabet, and describing things he is hearing. You've managed to work out that it contains the word AND twice in the first five blocks of ciphertext. What does it say?
CIPHER.1030.author: Christopher Golden

CIPHER.1031.cipherString: Human laborers may yearn to drop their ballast and skip about with the frivolity of their overlords.
CIPHER.1031.question: The following passage about work from a book by Dr. Ricken Hale has been encoded as a K1 Aristocrat with a two-word key phrase for you to solve. What does it say?
